events.text:
You are an expert full-stack developer specializing in rapid vibe-coding with Next.js 15 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, PostgreSQL, and Drizzle ORM. Build me a production-ready personal social media poster app called "QuadPoster" that matches exactly the functionality, UI flow, and architecture shown in the live stream recap below.

### TECH STACK (non-negotiable)
- Next.js 15 App Router + React Server Components
- TypeScript
- Tailwind + shadcn/ui + lucide-react icons
- Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL (use Railway Postgres)
- NextAuth.js (for simple credentials login – username/password for now; we’ll add OAuth later)
- Redis (Railway) for any background jobs if needed
- Deploy to Railway (one web service + optional worker)
- Dark mode ON by default (system preference fallback)
- All pages must be fully responsive and look polished

### CORE REQUIREMENTS (single user for now – multi-user later optional)
The app lets me prepare ONE piece of content and automatically adapt it for X, YouTube (long + Shorts), TikTok, Facebook, LinkedIn. I can review, queue, bulk-schedule, and publish. It also exposes a full REST API so my external Clawbot agent can push content programmatically.

### DATABASE SCHEMA (Drizzle)
Create these tables exactly:

1. users (id, username, password_hash, created_at)
2. social_accounts (id, user_id, platform: 'x'|'youtube'|'tiktok'|'facebook'|'linkedin', account_name, oauth_tokens jsonb, connected_at)
3. media (id, user_id, file_url, file_type, filename, usage_count, created_at)
4. posts (id, user_id, title, raw_content text, content_type: 'post'|'story'|'reel'|'short'|'article', tags text[], status: 'draft'|'queued'|'approved'|'scheduled'|'published'|'archived', created_at, updated_at)
5. post_platforms (id, post_id, platform, formatted_content text, platform_post_id text, posted_at timestamp, status: 'pending'|'posted'|'failed')
6. schedules (id, post_id, scheduled_for timestamp, platform, created_at)
7. api_keys (id, user_id, key text unique, name, expires_at, created_at)

Seed the database on first run with:
- One test user (username: "admin", password: "123")
- Fake social accounts for all 5 platforms
- 15 sample posts (mix of statuses + media)
- Several media files (use placeholder URLs)

### PAGES & UI (exact flow from the stream)
1. /login → simple form
2. /dashboard – overview cards + recent posts + quick "New Post" button
3. /posts/new – rich form:
   - Title
   - Raw content (textarea + simple rich text toolbar)
   - Content type dropdown (post/story/reel/short/article) – changes available fields
   - Media uploader (drag & drop, multiple)
   - Tags (multi-select)
   - Live preview pane on the right showing how it will look on each platform (character count warnings, shortened versions, etc.)
   - Buttons: Save Draft | Submit to Queue
4. /queue – list of “queued” posts with Approve/Reject + bulk approve
5. /schedule – calendar view + powerful bulk scheduler:
   - Filter by platform/status
   - Select multiple posts that have never been posted
   - "Start date" picker
   - Presets: Morning Blitz (7am, 8am, 9am), Viral Mix (9am, 12pm, 5pm, 7pm), or custom time slots
   - Option: "Every X hours/days" or manual time picker per day
   - Checkbox: "Prevent double-posting on same platform"
   - Preview of resulting schedule before confirming
6. /media – gallery with usage count, "used in X posts", delete protection if linked to scheduled posts
7. /accounts – list of connected accounts (for now just show the seeded ones)
8. /settings – Generate/Regenerate API key + copy button
9. /api-docs – beautiful Swagger-style page showing every endpoint (use next-swagger-doc or just markdown + code blocks)

### API (REST + full documentation)
All routes under /api/v1, protected by API key in header `x-api-key`.

Endpoints (must be fully implemented):
- POST /api/v1/posts – create post (Clawbot uses this)
- GET /api/v1/posts?status=queued – list posts
- PATCH /api/v1/posts/:id/approve
- POST /api/v1/posts/:id/schedule
- POST /api/v1/bulk-schedule
- GET /api/v1/media
- POST /api/v1/media/upload
- DELETE /api/v1/media/:id (with safeguards)
- GET /api/v1/schedule/calendar

Return proper JSON with clear error messages.

### EXTRA POLISH FROM THE STREAM
- When creating a post, show character counts and auto-truncation previews for X (280), TikTok caption, etc.
- Platform icons light up when content is ready for that platform.
- Bulk schedule must show estimated posting days (e.g. "15 posts → 5 days").
- Archive view for old posts.
- Every delete action must warn if it affects scheduled posts.
- Dark theme everywhere, clean cards, smooth animations.

### DEPLOYMENT INSTRUCTIONS
After coding:
1. Create a private GitHub repo and push.
2. I will connect it to Railway – auto-deploy on main.
3. Provide exact Railway environment variables needed.
4. Give me the one-line command to run locally with Railway Postgres (use DATABASE_URL).

### DEVELOPMENT WORKFLOW
Build in phases. After each phase say “PHASE X COMPLETE – ready for next command”.

Phase 1: Project setup, Drizzle schema, auth, seed data, basic layout + dark mode
Phase 2: Dashboard, accounts, media library
Phase 3: New Post form + live multi-platform previews
Phase 4: Queue + approval flow
Phase 5: Schedule page + advanced bulk scheduler
Phase 6: Full REST API + API key system + /api-docs page
Phase 7: Polish, mobile responsiveness, final bug fixes, Railway deployment commands

Start with Phase 1 right now. Use the exact folder structure of a standard Next.js 15 app with Drizzle.

When you finish a phase, show me the key files changed and say “Ready for your next instruction”.

Begin.
Starting Phase 1 now. I'll build the project setup, schema, auth, seed data, layout, and dark mode.

PHASE 4: Queue page + approval flow (exactly as shown in the stream)

Create the full review/approval experience.

1. New route: src/app/(app)/queue/page.tsx
   - Server component that fetches all posts where status = 'queued' or 'draft' (show both but separate tabs)
   - Two tabs: “Drafts” and “Queued for Review”
   - Responsive table (use shadcn Table) with columns:
     - Title
     - Content type badge
     - Platforms (show icons for which platforms are targeted)
     - Media count
     - Created date
     - Actions: View | Approve | Reject
   - Click “View” opens a modal with full post details + live previews (reuse the preview logic from /posts/new)
   - Approve button → changes status to 'approved' and moves to schedule-ready
   - Reject button → changes status to 'archived' or back to 'draft' with optional note field
   - Bulk select (checkboxes) + Bulk Approve / Bulk Reject buttons at top

2. Server actions (add to src/app/(app)/posts/actions.ts)
   - approvePost(postId)
   - rejectPost(postId, reason?)

### Railway Deployment — Exact Steps

```bash
# 1. Create a private GitHub repo and push
cd quadposter
git init
git add .
git commit -m "QuadPoster v1.0 — Social Media Command Center"
gh repo create quadposter --private --push --source=.

# 2. Go to railway.com → New Project
#    - "Deploy from GitHub Repo" → select quadposter
#    - Click "Add Service" → "Database" → "PostgreSQL"

# 3. Set these environment variables on the web service:
#    DATABASE_URL    = ${{Postgres.DATABASE_URL}}
#    AUTH_SECRET     = (run: openssl rand -base64 32)
#    AUTH_TRUST_HOST = true

# 4. Railway auto-deploys. After first deploy, seed the DB:
railway run npm run db:push
railway run npm run db:seed

# 5. Your app is live at: https://quadposter-production-XXXX.up.railway.app
```
